mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a monthly basis, the discount rate applied to equities declines and financial conditions ease, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts are already fully priced or if equity risk premia widen faster than policy eases.
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in most triggered months or if forward returns are not higher than unconditional returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above its recent trend implies inflation pressure that forces tighter policy expectations and higher real yields, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when inflation rises but FEDFUNDS remains unchanged and markets treat the shock as transitory.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average by 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor income growth and lower expected consumer demand, which weighs on corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails if unemployment rises only because labor supply increases while payrolls remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than half of observations or if the sign of the average return is nonnegative over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue growth for listed firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if sales growth comes from price inflation rather than real volume gains.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is not associated with higher 20d SPX returns than baseline, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL remains subdued, the economy is improving through real output growth rather than inflation, which should lift equity earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if industrial output rises because of inventory rebuilding without broad demand follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if high INDPRO growth with flat CPIAUCSL does not outperform unconditional 60d returns, or if the conditional win rate is below 55% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signals broad labor-market strengthening, which supports household income and equity earnings and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll gains are concentrated in low-quality hiring while unemployment does not fall.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down combination is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 40% of occurrences or if average returns are not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior high level signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and slower cyclical momentum, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when housing weakness is offset by a simultaneous fall in FEDFUNDS that improves financial conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-decline triggers are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is nonnegative over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with rising CPIAUCSL pressures real yields upward and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails once inflation falls decisively and policy is no longer restrictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-policy episodes produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the sign of the conditional average return flips positive in a tightening regime.
